title: plagiopetalum serratum
side_effects: Plagiopetalum serratum can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing allergic reactions, including skin irritation, itching, and swelling. Ingestion of the plant can lead to stomach upset, nausea, and vomiting due to its toxic compounds. Prolonged exposure to the plant's essential oils may cause headaches, dizziness, and respiratory problems in some individuals. In severe cases, improper use of Plagiopetalum serratum can result in liver damage and kidney failure.
